modals.gapTitle: All countries — urban vs rural gap 2020 (largest first)

sections.continentalAvg: Continental avg — % with water access (34 countries)
sections.urbanRuralGap: Urban vs rural gap — biggest disparities in 2020

insights.progressDesc: The continental average climbed from ~44% in 2000 to ~54% in 2023 across the 34 countries with available data — meaningful progress, though coverage gaps for ~20 countries mean the true picture may be bleaker.
insights.twoSpeedDesc: Morocco, Uganda, and Tanzania each gained 30+ percentage points, while Central African Republic and DR Congo moved backward. The gap between leaders and laggards has grown wider.
insights.ruralDeficitDesc: In Gambia and Lesotho, the urban-rural gap exceeds 54 percentage points. Urban populations are often 3–7× more likely to have safe water access than their rural counterparts.

footer.left: Population using safely managed / at least basic drinking water services
